factors.data_availability: low — cirrhosis tissue is primarily from liver transplant programs (explants) or clinical biopsies, not commercial biobanks
factors.org_discoverability: low — AminoChain has only 1 org with 3 donors; academic liver banks (KUMC, MGH, Edinburgh/Bristol, NIDDK NASH CRN) require PubMed mining to surface
factors.etiology_specificity: high — etiology documentation (ALD/NASH/HBV/HCV/autoimmune) varies widely across biobanks; rarely pre-annotated in commercial registries
factors.staging_complexity: high — Child-Pugh score is a clinical score not captured in pathology databases; METAVIR F4 requires expert liver pathology review
factors.donor_pool: extremely thin on AminoChain (3 donors) — single org

expected_failure_modes: Pipeline treats 174 ProteoGenex specimens as 174 independent donors without flagging the 3-donor constraint
Etiology assumed to be documented because 63% have staging-like data — etiology and fibrosis stage are independent fields
Child-Pugh score assumed available from pathology report — it is a clinical composite score not typically in pathology databases
Pipeline finds only ProteoGenex and misses academic liver biobanks (NIDDK NASH CRN, KUMC, European NAFLD Registry) which are the primary valid sources
METAVIR F4 assumed from staging-like data without pathology confirmation
Pipeline may confuse HCC (hepatocellular carcinoma) cohorts with cirrhosis cohorts since cirrhosis is often the precursor disease in HCC series
